猜想.摘要: Best-available closure keeps 'ICT' ahead of 'TICT', but the main unresolved gap remains: The unresolved main gap is the absence of a molecule-specific closure-grade discriminator showing whether twisting in this molecule meaningfully decouples donor and acceptor segments enough to justify a TICT-centered emissive interpretation over ICT. Best available classification is ICT. The strongest positive chain is the structured donor-acceptor architecture, low-gap excitation context, external close-family precedent consistent with ICT/AIE behavior, and the lack of any molecule-specific twist-dominant signal emerging from the bounded torsion-sensitive internal route. TICT remains credible and not fully excluded, so the conclusion is best-available rather than decisive.

最终证据摘要: Top1 is ICT at 0.60 and top2 is TICT at 0.22. I interpret the verifier evidence as mixed but net supportive of ICT: it adds close-family external precedent spanning both ICT-like donor-acceptor/AIE behavior and TICT-related discriminators, yet it does not provide an exact-compound or molecule-specific twist-dominant signature that would overturn the current lead. This verifier pass does not reduce portfolio coverage debt because portfolio screening was already complete; instead it supports pairwise contraction by sharpening the ICT-versus-TICT comparison. No still-credible hypothesis remains untested, but TICT remains only indirectly weakened rather than decisively ruled out. The case therefore remains legitimately in pairwise contraction, not portfolio screening. Closure justification is still collecting rather than sufficient or blocked: verifier supplementation for the current pair is sufficient, but the needed final closure-grade discriminator is still missing. The case is not ready for decisive finalize, and not yet appropriate for best-available finalize because the main unresolved pairwise gap can still be addressed within the remaining round budget.

Best-available closure keeps 'ICT' ahead of 'TICT', but the main unresolved gap remains: The unresolved main gap is the absence of a molecule-specific closure-grade discriminator showing whether twisting in this molecule meaningfully decouples donor and acceptor segments enough to justify a TICT-centered emissive interpretation over ICT. Best available classification is ICT. The strongest positive chain is the structured donor-acceptor architecture, low-gap excitation context, external close-family precedent consistent with ICT/AIE behavior, and the lack of any molecule-specific twist-dominant signal emerging from the bounded torsion-sensitive internal route. TICT remains credible and not fully excluded, so the conclusion is best-available rather than decisive.

Verifier supplementation for ICT versus TICT is sufficient and high-information. It is based on close-family donor-acceptor triarylamine/cyanopyridine and TICT-discriminator precedent, with no exact-compound hit. The net effect is pairwise sharpening rather than decisive resolution.

Closure justification is still not decisive because the desired molecule-specific discriminator remains missing. Best-available closure is justified because portfolio screening is complete, verifier supplementation for the active pair is sufficient, and the unresolved gap can be stated explicitly.

Portfolio screening remains complete. The new verifier pass does not reopen any coverage debt; instead it strengthens pairwise interpretation within the already-screened ICT versus TICT contest. TICT and neutral aromatic remain directly screened, ESIPT is dropped with reason, and unknown remains residual only.

Macro capability has already exhausted its role because portfolio debt is closed. Microscopic pairwise work changed observables several times but has now plateaued: CT extraction underdelivered, geometry parsing missed torsion/planarity/conjugation descriptors, and raw inspection was inventory-only. The verifier provided the highest recent information gain and is already sufficient for supplementation, but as close-family evidence it cannot by itself create decisive molecule-specific closure.

New verifier evidence retrieved 4 close-family evidence cards for ICT versus TICT, covering AIE, ICT, RIR, triarylamine, donor-acceptor emitters, solvatochromism, TICT, DMABN-like discriminator themes, orthogonal geometry, dark state, dual emission, viscosity, temperature, and quantum yield. Internal evidence from torsion snapshots and follow-up parsing did not reveal a closure-grade twist/conjugation disruption signal, but also did not produce a molecule-specific TICT-favoring signature.
